architecture_patterns.mapping: Map public APIs as systems, declaration objects as schemas, runtime guards as gates, and tests/docs as release surfaces.
architecture_patterns.name: Framework repo
architecture_patterns.redaction: Usually safe to share source paths; do not invent production deployment details.
architecture_patterns.works_for: Web frameworks, SDKs, CLI frameworks, and runtime libraries

architecture_patterns.mapping: Show how developer declarations become generated contracts and how the contract is validated.
architecture_patterns.name: Generated contract surface
architecture_patterns.redaction: Use public sample schemas or fake app examples.
architecture_patterns.works_for: OpenAPI, GraphQL, protobuf, and typed client generation

artifacts.name: FastAPI Package
artifacts.purpose: Public framework package imported by application authors.

artifacts.name: Routing Source
artifacts.purpose: Connects declared routes to request handling.

artifacts.name: Dependency Source
artifacts.purpose: Resolves dependency graphs and request-time inputs.

artifacts.name: OpenAPI Source
artifacts.purpose: Builds OpenAPI artifacts from application declarations.

artifacts.name: Documentation Site
artifacts.purpose: Explains framework usage and examples for application authors.

artifacts.name: Test Suite
artifacts.purpose: Protects behavior across routing, validation, docs, security, and compatibility.

bigger_picture: This example shows how to map a framework repo. The system is not one deployed application; it is a reusable engine that lets application authors declare routes, dependencies, models, security, and documentation contracts. A reviewer should follow how a developer declaration becomes runtime routing, validation, response handling, and OpenAPI documentation.

edges.why: Docs teach developers how to use the declaration surface.

gates.name: Declaration Gate
gates.risk_boundary: Invalid route declarations should fail before they become runtime behavior.

rules.if: endpoint callable and method/path are valid

gates.name: Dependency Resolution Gate
gates.risk_boundary: Endpoint code should not run with unresolved dependencies.

rules.if: dependency raises or security requirement fails

gates.name: Request And Response Validation Gate
gates.risk_boundary: Payload shape should match declared contracts.

gates.name: OpenAPI Contract Gate
gates.risk_boundary: Generated docs should reflect declared API behavior.

rules.if: route metadata and schemas are complete
documentation metadata is missing or inconsistent

gates.name: Test And Release Gate
gates.risk_boundary: Framework behavior changes should be protected by tests and maintainer review.

rules.if: tests pass and review accepts compatibility impact

known_boundaries: This is a public educational map, not an official FastAPI maintainer audit.
It maps framework behavior, not a specific deployed FastAPI application.
Runtime details may evolve as the upstream repo changes.
A real audit should inspect the exact commit, CI status, tests, and release notes.
one_line: FastAPI turns Python type hints and path-operation declarations into validated API runtime behavior and OpenAPI contracts.

rebuild_recipe.goal: Check the FastAPI public repo manifest.
Generate the FastAPI system review report.

review_questions: How does a Python route declaration become runtime route behavior?
Which gates prevent unresolved dependencies or invalid payloads from reaching endpoint logic?
How does generated OpenAPI stay aligned with runtime behavior?
Which tests protect compatibility for routing, validation, dependencies, security, and docs?
What should a reviewer inspect if auditing a specific FastAPI application built on top of this framework?

schemas.name: PathOperationDeclaration
schemas.privacy_notes: This is a framework contract, not user production data.
schemas.purpose: Describes what an application author declares when registering an API endpoint.

schemas.name: DependencySpec
schemas.purpose: Represents dependencies that must be resolved before an endpoint runs.

schemas.name: ValidationContract
schemas.purpose: Connects Python type hints and model definitions to request and response validation.

schemas.name: OpenAPIContract
schemas.purpose: The generated machine-readable API contract used by docs and clients.

schemas.name: TestScenario
schemas.purpose: A public test scenario that protects framework behavior.

scope: A public-safe system map of the FastAPI open-source repository based on public source directories and documentation.

source_links.label: GitHub repository
source_links.notes: Primary public source used for repo identity and source paths.

source_links.label: FastAPI documentation
source_links.notes: Public docs for framework concepts and user-facing behavior.

source_links.label: Public OpenAPI docs concept
source_links.notes: High-level feature surface for generated API documentation and validation.

systems.ideal_target: Small Python declarations produce predictable API behavior.
systems.lifecycle: developer code -> path operation declaration -> route registry
systems.name: Developer Declaration Surface
systems.purpose: The public API application authors use to declare routes, models, dependencies, and metadata.
systems.truth_boundary: A declaration is intent; runtime gates still validate requests and dependencies.

systems.ideal_target: Every request lands on the right endpoint contract or fails clearly.
systems.name: Routing Layer
systems.purpose: Matches incoming HTTP requests to declared path operations.
systems.truth_boundary: Route matching does not mean endpoint execution is safe yet.

systems.ideal_target: Dependency behavior is traceable and test-covered.
systems.lifecycle: route match -> dependency graph -> endpoint arguments
systems.name: Dependency Engine
systems.purpose: Resolves nested dependencies, security requirements, and request-time inputs.
systems.truth_boundary: Dependencies can block execution before endpoint logic runs.

systems.lifecycle: endpoint args -> endpoint result -> response model
systems.name: Validation And Serialization
systems.purpose: Validates request inputs and serializes endpoint responses according to declared contracts.
systems.truth_boundary: Validation is bounded by declared models and framework compatibility behavior.

systems.ideal_target: Docs and runtime contracts stay aligned.
systems.lifecycle: path operation declarations -> OpenAPI document -> docs UI
systems.name: OpenAPI And Docs Engine
systems.purpose: Turns route declarations and schemas into machine-readable OpenAPI and human documentation.
systems.truth_boundary: Generated docs describe declared framework behavior, not an external production service.

systems.ideal_target: Framework changes are reviewable, tested, and documented.
systems.lifecycle: source change -> test scenario -> release candidate
systems.name: Quality And Release Loop
systems.purpose: Uses tests and review to protect framework compatibility.
systems.truth_boundary: This public map does not replace maintainer CI or release policy.

title: FastAPI Public Repo System Review Graph

walkthroughs.name: One route from declaration to response
walkthroughs.story: A developer declares a path operation. FastAPI registers the route, resolves dependencies for an incoming request, validates inputs, executes endpoint logic, validates/serializes the response, and exposes the route in OpenAPI.

walkthroughs.name: How to audit without production data
walkthroughs.story: A reviewer does not need a real app database. They inspect the framework path: declaration, routing, dependency resolution, validation, OpenAPI output, tests, and docs.

workflows.name: Declare Path Operation
workflows.purpose: Create the API contract from Python code.

workflows.name: Match Request
workflows.purpose: Route incoming requests to the correct declared endpoint.

workflows.name: Resolve Dependencies
workflows.purpose: Build endpoint arguments and enforce dependency/security requirements.

workflows.name: Validate And Execute
workflows.purpose: Run endpoint logic after validation and serialize the result.

workflows.name: Publish OpenAPI Contract
workflows.purpose: Expose route behavior as docs and machine-readable contract.
